prompt:
A close-up view of a small 220 ohm through-hole resistor with colored bands, resting on a light wooden desk next to a breadboard. The resistor’s leads are slightly bent as if ready to be inserted, and in the background a simple LED and a few jumper wires are visible, suggesting that this part is commonly used to limit current to that LED in a basic circuit. The focus is on the resistor body and its color bands so it’s clearly identifiable as a specific component.

Near-future solarpunk electronics workbench aesthetic with soft, even lighting and a clean, organized feel. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering, no brand logos or legible text anywhere on components. The composition should make it obvious that this is a small, single resistor item in the inventory rather than a full circuit.
